items.title: Lucian Freud’s Dozing Nude
items.summary: Remembering Julio Le Parc, a dispatch from art shows in abandoned homes in Seattle, and more.

items.title: Meticulously Detailed Natural Specimens by Marisa Aragón Ware Emerge from Paper
items.summary: "Because paper is such an everyday material, there’s something especially powerful about transforming it into something unexpected." Do stories and artists like this matter to you? Become a Colossal M…

items.title: 15 Art Shows to See in Los Angeles This Summer
items.summary: A romp through early punk culture, Odilon Redon’s dreamy portraiture, Willie Birch’s papier-mâché odes to New Orleans, Samella Lewis’s visions in woodcut, and more.

items.title: Seattle Artists Breathe Life Into Houses Slated for Demolition
items.summary: “We want to act as a little eddy in the stream of gentrification, giving these houses one last burst of life before they’re gone,” one of the organizers of “Once Removed” told Hyperallergic.
